---
# {{ site.title }}

> {{ site.tagline | default: site.description }}

This file is a guide for Large Language Models (LLMs) to navigate and
summarize the content on this site. All links are absolute and point to
the canonical, human-readable versions of each page.

## About the Author

Yangyi Liu — B.S. Molecular and Cell Biology, UC San Diego '26 (Cum Laude).
Lives near the UCSD campus.

**Current work:** [AmadeusBio.ai](https://www.amadeusbio.ai) (2026–present)
— developing agentic systems for bioinformatic analysis. Post-graduation
focus is agentic applications in biological research.

**Ethos:** Chose not to enter the conventional rat race after graduating.
Wants to do work that is valuable both instrumentally and in a Kantian
sense. Sees universities and many academic institutions as significantly
behind on AI knowledge, and is skeptical of senior professors who dismiss
LLM hallucinations without understanding how LLMs work.

### Interests

- **AI** — Believes technology will transform humanity, starting with the
  younger generation. Active in machine-learning, neuroscience, and
  biology seminars in the UCSD area; pivoting toward groups he considers
  productive on AI and away from those he considers counterproductive.
- **Art** — Likes illustrative anime art; draws as a hobby. Recently
  played *Steins;Gate* and considers visual novels (Galgames) a modern
  equivalent of Shakespearean plays — analogous to how Athenians regarded
  Sophocles. A phenomenology course on Heidegger echoes through how he
  reads them.
- **Crows** — Feeds crows on the UCSD campus as a counterweight to
  anthropocentric narratives.

### How AI has changed his life

Micro:
- Listens to papers via [Paper2Audio](https://www.paper2audio.com/) while
  walking, biking, or sitting — covers roughly an order of magnitude more
  papers per week than he used to read.
- Treats LLM chat as his primary learning tool; abandoned flashcards and
  note-taking once course materials became fully digital.
- Uses LLMs to cut through SEO junk in search — especially valuable on the
  Chinese-language internet, where useful information is often buried.

Macro:
- Questions the value of memorization-heavy assessments such as the MCAT
  now that knowledge lookup is trivial; prefers skill-based learning.
- Self-directs study in novel areas comfortably; lost his "ivory tower"
  dream after observing how complacent many academics are about AI.

### Recommended learning resources he points to

- [MIT Missing Semester](https://missing.csail.mit.edu/)
- [Anthropic Academy](https://anthropic.skilljar.com/)

### Contact

Email **lyangyilliu@gmail.com**, plus Discord, GitHub
([starryark](https://github.com/starryark)), LinkedIn, and Steam — all
linked from the sidebar of the site.
